---
title: "Other Output Formats"
---

One of Quarto's biggest advantages: **one `.qmd` file → multiple output formats**.
You write the content once and change only one line to get PDF, HTML, or Word.

---

## Change the format

Open your `.qmd` file and edit the `format:` line in the header block at the top.

**For HTML** (no extra tools needed, works in any browser):
```yaml
---
title: "Exercise Sheet 1"
format: html
---
```

**For Word** (`.docx`, can be edited in Microsoft Word):
```yaml
---
title: "Exercise Sheet 1"
format: docx
---
```

**For PDF** (requires TinyTeX — see [What you need](beg_exercise_1.qmd)):
```yaml
---
title: "Exercise Sheet 1"
format: pdf
---
```

Then run `quarto render` in the terminal. Quarto produces the file in the format you chose.

---

## Produce all formats at once

You can list multiple formats — Quarto will render all of them in one go:

```yaml
---
title: "Exercise Sheet 1"
format:
  html: default
  pdf: default
  docx: default
---
```

Run `quarto render` once and you get three output files.

---

::: {.callout-note}
## Official Documentation
See all available formats and their options at **[quarto.org/docs/output-formats/all-formats.html](https://quarto.org/docs/output-formats/all-formats.html)**.
:::
